pi_items.verified_value: SPY, AAPL, MSFT are real trading instruments
pi_items.source: Yahoo Finance, NASDAQ, NYSE Arca official listings. SPY is SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust on NYSE Arca. AAPL is Apple Inc. on NASDAQ. MSFT is Microsoft Corporation on NASDAQ.

pi_items.verified_value: RSI is the Relative Strength Index, a standard technical analysis momentum oscillator
pi_items.source: Official RSI documentation from StockCharts.com, Wikipedia, Fidelity, and NinjaTrader support documentation confirm RSI is a real indicator used across all major trading platforms

pi_items.verified_value: 14 is the standard period for RSI, recommended by J. Welles Wilder who created the indicator
pi_items.source: StockCharts.com RSI documentation, Wikipedia article on Relative Strength Index, and Fidelity technical analysis guide all confirm that 14 is the default and most widely used period

pi_items.verified_value: 70 is the standard overbought threshold for RSI
pi_items.source: StockCharts.com, Fidelity, Charles Schwab, and Wikipedia all confirm that RSI values above 70 indicate overbought conditions, established by J. Welles Wilder

pi_items.verified_value: 30 is the standard oversold threshold for RSI
pi_items.source: StockCharts.com, Fidelity, Charles Schwab, and Wikipedia all confirm that RSI values below 30 indicate oversold conditions, established by J. Welles Wilder

pi_items.verified_value: Red for overbought conditions is a reasonable color choice, though not universally standard
pi_items.source: Web search of trading indicator color conventions shows varied implementations: some use Red for oversold/Green for overbought, others use Green for overbought/Red for oversold. The task uses Red for overbought (potential sell signal, caution) and Green for oversold (potential buy opportunity), which is semantically reasonable.

pi_items.verified_value: Green for oversold conditions is a reasonable color choice, though not universally standard
pi_items.source: Web search of trading indicator color conventions shows varied implementations. Green typically signifies positive/buy opportunity (oversold = undervalued/buy), which aligns with the task's choice.

pi_items.verified_value: Market Analyzer is a confirmed feature in NinjaTrader 8 that allows display of real-time quotes and indicator values
pi_items.source: Official NinjaTrader 8 support documentation at ninjatrader.com/support/helpguides/nt8/ confirms Market Analyzer exists and can display custom columns including technical indicators

pi_items.metadata_value: RSI column with conditional formatting (from description)
pi_items.verified_value: RSI can be added as a column to NinjaTrader Market Analyzer with conditional formatting capability
pi_items.source: NinjaTrader 8 documentation and forum discussions confirm that RSI indicators can be added as Market Analyzer columns and support conditional cell color formatting

privileged_info_summary: The task involves real financial instruments (SPY, AAPL, MSFT) and the standard RSI (Relative Strength Index) technical indicator with the industry-standard 14-period setting and overbought/oversold thresholds of 70 and 30 respectively, as established by indicator creator J. Welles Wilder. These are all authentic components used in professional trading platforms. The NinjaTrader 8 Market Analyzer is a real feature that supports adding RSI columns with custom conditional formatting. The underlying data consists of real historical OHLCV daily data from January 2023 for these three securities, not synthetic data.
